settings.title: Settings
settings.vscodeEditorTypeHint: Choose which VS Code edition to use when opening workspaces
settings.createAgentTerminalHint: When enabled, new workspaces will include an Agent Terminal.
settings.autoRunAgentCommandHint: Automatically execute the agent command (e.g., `claude`) when creating an Agent Terminal.
settings.allowBypassPermissionsHint: When enabled, switching to bypassPermissions mode in Claude Agent Panel will not show a confirmation dialog. Use with caution.
settings.agentCustomArgsHint: Add custom CLI arguments for each agent. These are appended to the agent command when launching terminals or executing work orders.
settings.collapseToolOutputs: Collapse all tool outputs by default
settings.collapseToolOutputsHint: When enabled, all tool outputs are collapsed by default. Click to expand. Read-only tools (Read, Glob, Grep) are always collapsed regardless of this setting.
settings.defaultModelHint: Model ID used for new Agent sessions. Leave empty to use SDK default.
settings.defaultEffortHint: Effort level used for new Agent sessions. Higher effort = more thorough but slower.
settings.checkForUpdatesHint: Periodically check GitHub for new releases and show a notification when an update is available.
settings.minimizeToTray: Minimize to system tray on close
settings.minimizeToTrayHint: When enabled, closing the window will minimize it to the system tray instead of quitting. Right-click the tray icon to fully quit.
settings.enableDevToolsHint: Enable F12 to open Developer Tools in packaged builds. DevTools is always available in development mode.
settings.showDockBadge: Show pending count on Dock icon
settings.showDockBadgeHint: Display a badge number on the Dock icon when workspaces have pending actions (permission requests, user questions).
settings.notifyOnCompleteHint: Send a system notification when an Agent session finishes its task.
settings.notifyOnlyBackground: Only notify when window is not focused
settings.notifyOnlyBackgroundHint: Suppress notifications when BAT is in the foreground.
settings.loggingHint: These logs capture the app's last known state during crashes. Please attach them when reporting bugs.
settings.statuslineHint: Drag to reorder, toggle visibility. Hover for description. Use L/C/R for alignment. Use > for separator.
settings.globalEnvVarsHint: Global environment variables applied to ALL workspaces. Workspace-specific variables (⚙ button) will override these.
settings.remoteAccessHint: Allow other BAT instances or mobile devices to remotely connect and control this host. See
settings.serverWarning: ⚠ After starting, any device with the Token can fully control BAT on this host. Make sure your network environment is secure.
settings.mobileConnectHint: Generate a QR Code for remote devices to scan and connect, allowing remote control of BAT on this host. You can also connect via another Better Agent Terminal using a Remote Profile. Recommended to use with
settings.qrWarning: ⚠ This will start a WebSocket Server allowing external connections. Make sure your network environment is secure before using.
settings.lanOnly: LAN only — phone must be on the same network
settings.footerNote: Changes are saved automatically. Font changes apply immediately to all terminals.

claude.yesDontAskAgain: Yes, don't ask again for this session
claude.yesDontAskAgainForCommands: Yes, and don't ask again for {{commands}} commands
claude.tellClaudeInstead: Tell Claude what to do instead
claude.model1mHint: 1M context has lower cache efficiency via SDK. For cost-sensitive workloads, consider using Claude CLI directly.
claude.v2ModelChangeWarning: Switching models in V2 will recreate the session and re-apply context, which may incur additional token costs. Continue?
claude.v1Model1mWarning: 1M context models in Claude Agent (V1) may cause significant unnecessary token consumption due to low cache efficiency (~25%). Consider using CLI or Claude Agent V2 for better cache performance. Continue?
claude.v2ModelListHint: Switching model in V2 will recreate the session and re-apply context, which may consume additional tokens. Please switch with caution.
claude.v1Model1mHint: 1M context models may cause significant unnecessary token consumption in V1 due to low cache efficiency (~25%). Consider using CLI or Claude Agent V2 window instead.
claude.v1v2Help:
V1 vs V2 Differences:

[V1 - Query Mode]
• Each message spawns a new CLI process and rebuilds from JSONL
• Cache efficiency ~25% (high token waste)
• Supports: effort, plugins, systemPrompt
• Stable, fully featured

[V2 - Session Mode]
• Persistent session, no process restart between messages
• Cache efficiency ~84% (significant token savings)
• Does NOT yet support: effort, plugins, systemPrompt
• Experimental, may have limitations

V2 is recommended for cost-sensitive or long conversations.
claude.forkSession: Fork: create a new tab from current conversation

claude.placeholderInterrupted: Type to continue, Esc to stop...
claude.placeholderStreaming: Press Esc to pause, double-Esc to stop...
